briefing.headline: Rig up a Flipping Jig — aggressive pre-spawn approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Flipping Jig is a strong choice for pre-spawn with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.color: Alabama craw with double header trailer
primaryApproach.retrieve: Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, dock, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: When wind pushes fish from top of cover down to the base as a current break, switch from swimbait to a 1/2-oz jig with Cleanup Craw and drag it over those places — fish reposition low on cove
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, dock, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: For pitching into grass holes — 3/8oz tungsten weight with a 3/0 Mustad grip pin hook (the pin keeps the bait from sliding down). 5-inch Ocho worm. Blue craw or green pumpkin. Making it fall straight is critical — the Texas rig glides down cl...

Palaniuk: 'The spot had a riprap bank with a steep drop, then a trough that came up onto a hard bottom ridge, tapered back off into another trough. The trough made a horseshoe. A 40-yard section where the fish wanted to live, with a 25-foot hole t...

Wheeler: The Punch Out Craw has five manifestations - full glide bait on 4/0 hook and 1/2-oz sinker, separated claws for football jig or Biffle Hardhead, trimmed small craw for swim jig/flipping jig/chatterbait/shakyhead, shortened torso for even ...

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal pre-spawn bass are aggressive and moving shallow. Expect fish in the 4-8ft range on secondary points and channel swings. Falling pressure pulls fish up from staging areas — target the first breakline off spawning flats.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If bites are subtle, try a lighter jig weight — slower fall rate can be the difference
